description: badly damaged A tedious law suit fol lowed in which Wallach employed Reverdy Johnson and other highpiiced lawyers The rejults of the law suit were not satis factory and Wallach tiring of fruit farm ing under such unfavorable conditions abandoned the eastern shore of Maryland and turned his attention again to his first lovethe soil of Virginda He bought a fine farm in the beautiful elevated country near Culpeper Court House and resided there until his death in December 1871 at the age of fiftynine Wallach was an interesting personality Vivacious restless overflowing with vital ity always on the move he was one of the bestknown figures in Washington life The freedom with which he expressed his opin ions frequently involved him in personal collisions He had a street light of the John Phoenix order with Extra Billy Smith of Virginia so called from a claim he had pushed for extra allowances on a mail contract Wallach had another sharp tight with John P Heiss of the tnion Val lach was always game and never shirked a fight no matter how heavily he was over matched in weight or muscle Vallachs sayings and doings were much in the public mind
